acne.description: Acne is a chronic inflammatory skin condition causing pimples, blackheads, and cysts, primarily affecting the face, chest, and back. It occurs when hair follicles become clogged with oil and dead skin cells.
acne.duration: Typically develops over 2–6 weeks; chronic cases can persist for months to years without treatment.
precautions: Wash affected areas with a gentle, oil-free cleanser twice daily
Avoid touching, picking, or squeezing pimples to prevent scarring
Use non-comedogenic (non-pore-clogging) makeup and skincare products
Change pillowcases every 1–2 days to reduce bacteria transfer
Stay hydrated and maintain a balanced, low-glycemic diet
Manage stress through exercise, sleep, and relaxation techniques
care_tips: Apply a benzoyl peroxide (2.5–5%) or salicylic acid cleanser
Use retinoids (adapalene gel) for blackheads and whiteheads
Consider niacinamide serum to reduce inflammation and redness
Apply non-comedogenic SPF 30+ sunscreen daily
Consult a dermatologist for prescription antibiotics or isotretinoin in severe cases

eczema.description: Eczema is a chronic inflammatory skin condition characterized by dry, itchy, red, and cracked skin. It is common in children but can affect any age group and often co-exists with asthma and hay fever.
eczema.duration: Can develop within days of trigger exposure; flare-ups last 1–3 weeks. Chronic eczema may persist for years.
precautions: Identify and strictly avoid personal triggers (soaps, dust, pet dander, certain foods)
Keep skin constantly moisturised — apply emollient within 3 minutes of bathing
Use mild, fragrance-free and dye-free soaps, detergents, and skincare products
Wear soft, breathable fabrics (cotton) and avoid rough or synthetic materials
Keep fingernails short and clean to minimise skin damage from scratching
Maintain indoor humidity at 45–55% using a humidifier
care_tips: Apply a thick, fragrance-free moisturiser (e.g., CeraVe, Eucerin) at least twice daily
Use over-the-counter 1% hydrocortisone cream for mild flare-ups
Take lukewarm (not hot) baths for 10–15 minutes and pat skin dry gently
Apply prescription topical corticosteroids or calcineurin inhibitors as directed
Consider antihistamines to relieve itch and improve sleep quality

psoriasis.description: Psoriasis is an autoimmune disease that accelerates the skin cell life cycle, causing cells to build up rapidly on the skin surface and form scales and red patches that can be itchy and sometimes painful.
psoriasis.duration: Plaques can develop over 1–2 weeks. Psoriasis is a lifelong condition with cycles of flare-ups (weeks to months) and remission.
precautions: Avoid known triggers: stress, infections, certain medications (beta-blockers, lithium), alcohol, and smoking
Protect skin from injury — even minor cuts can trigger the Koebner phenomenon
Moisturise skin daily to reduce scaling, cracking, and discomfort
Limit alcohol consumption as it can worsen flare-ups and reduce treatment effectiveness
Eat an anti-inflammatory diet rich in fruits, vegetables, and omega-3 fatty acids
Manage stress with yoga, meditation, or counselling
care_tips: Apply coal tar or salicylic acid shampoos/creams to reduce scaling
Use prescription topical corticosteroids or vitamin D analogues (calcipotriol)
Consider controlled UVB phototherapy under medical supervision
Biologics (TNF inhibitors, IL-17/23 blockers) are highly effective for moderate-to-severe cases
Regular dermatology follow-ups are essential to adjust treatment plans

nail_fungus.description: Nail fungus is a common fungal infection that begins as a white or yellow spot under the tip of the fingernail or toenail. As it deepens, it causes the nail to discolour, thicken, and crumble at the edges.
nail_fungus.duration: Develops gradually over months. Nails grow slowly, so full treatment and nail regrowth can take 6–18 months.
precautions: Keep nails short, dry, and clean at all times
Wear moisture-wicking socks and change them daily or when sweaty
Wear waterproof sandals or shoes in public pools, locker rooms, and showers
Do not share nail clippers, files, or footwear with others
Disinfect nail tools with 70% isopropyl alcohol after each use
Choose breathable footwear materials (leather, mesh) to reduce moisture build-up
care_tips: Apply OTC antifungal nail lacquer (ciclopirox) or cream (clotrimazole) daily
Soak feet in a diluted white vinegar solution (1:2 ratio) for 20 minutes daily
Prescription oral antifungals (terbinafine, itraconazole) are most effective for severe cases
Laser therapy is an emerging option for resistant nail fungus
Trim and thin infected nail before applying topical treatments for better penetration
